title: Social Security Survivor Benefits: Who Qualifies
summary: Survivor benefits can provide meaningful support to a spouse, children, or other dependents — worth understanding even amid the busier tasks.
category: After a Loss II: Notifications & Accounts
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Social Security Survivor Benefits: Who Qualifies | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Survivor benefits can provide meaningful support to a spouse, children, or other dependents — worth understanding even amid the busier tasks.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Surviving spouses, minor children, and sometimes dependent parents may qualify.
Age and marriage-duration rules affect spousal eligibility.
Applying promptly matters, since some benefits aren't fully retroactive.

paragraphs: Eligibility can extend to a surviving spouse (sometimes as early as age 60, or younger if caring for a minor child), unmarried children under 18 (or older if disabled), and in some cases dependent parents of the deceased.

Because some survivor benefits are not paid retroactively to the date of death, applying as soon as reasonably possible — rather than waiting until other tasks are finished — can matter for the total benefit received.

nextSteps: Contact Social Security to ask about survivor benefit eligibility specifically.
Apply promptly rather than waiting for other tasks to finish.
Ask about documentation needed (marriage certificate, birth certificates) in advance.
disclaimer: General information — the Social Security Administration can confirm specific eligibility for your family's situation.
